docs.title: Blaze Dev Kit
docs.text:
AI-Powered WordPress Development Toolkit

The Blaze Dev Kit transforms Claude Code into a WordPress expert for your Kinsta-hosted site. Deploy once, and get automated maintenance, performance optimization, security auditing, and specialist AI agents - all pre-configured and ready to work.

v0.1.0

Get Started View on GitHub

docs.title: WordPress Maintenance
docs.text:
Automated core, plugin, and theme updates with maintenance mode, Slack notifications, and premium plugin management.

Command: /wpm

docs.title: Site Optimization
docs.text:
Autonomous performance optimization targeting 90+ Lighthouse scores across PageSpeed, Accessibility, and Best Practices.

Commands: /pagespeed /accessibility /bestpractices

docs.title: Web Development
docs.text:
Full WordPress development with safety guardrails, specialist skills for blocks, themes, plugins, REST API, and more.

Skills: 14 specialist knowledge modules

docs.title: SEO & Marketing
docs.text:
Technical SEO auditing, schema markup, and performance-focused optimization for search visibility.

Skills: SEO specialist knowledge

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Download Kit] --> B[Upload to Kinsta]
    B --> C[Extract .claude/]
    C --> D[Delete zip]
    D --> E[Run /init]
    E --> F[Claude Code Ready]
  1. Download the latest release zip from GitHub
  2. Upload to your Kinsta site root
  3. Extract - creates the .claude/ directory
  4. Initialize - run /init to generate a site-specific CLAUDE.md
  5. Work - use any command: /wpm, /pagespeed, /accessibility, and more

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
The kit includes:

Component Count Purpose Safety Hooks 2 Block destructive commands and protected file edits Commands 9 Slash commands for maintenance, optimization, auditing Skills 14 Specialist knowledge modules (WordPress, PHP, security, etc.) Agents 7 AI subagents with model routing (opus/sonnet/haiku) Scripts 9 Shell tools for testing, caching, updating Templates 6 Service-specific bases for CLAUDE.md generation

docs.title: Repository Structure
docs.text:
The Blaze Dev Kit repo separates concerns into four strictly enforced categories:

blaze-dev-kit/
├── deploy/                          # [DEPLOY] Packaged into downloadable zip
│   └── .claude/
│       ├── settings.json            # Claude Code permissions + hooks
│       ├── hooks/PreToolUse/        # Safety enforcement (2 hooks)
│       ├── commands/                # Slash commands (9 commands)
│       ├── skills/                  # Specialist knowledge (14 skills)
│       ├── agents/                  # AI subagents (7 agents)
│       ├── scripts/                 # Shell tools (9 scripts)
│       └── templates/               # CLAUDE.md + service bases (6 templates)
│
├── docs/                            # [DOCS] MkDocs Material → GitHub Pages
│   └── docs/
│       ├── getting-started/         # Installation, init, requirements
│       ├── services/                # WPM, optimization, dev, SEO
│       ├── workflows/               # Setup, updating, contributing, releasing
│       ├── reference/               # Commands, skills, agents, scripts
│       ├── roadmap/                 # Product roadmap
│       └── faq/                     # Frequently asked questions
│
├── qa/                              # [QA] Playwright visual regression (local only)
├── eval/                            # [REPO] Skill evaluation scenarios
├── scripts/                         # [REPO] Zip builder, verifier, scaffolder
├── .github/                         # [REPO] CI/CD workflows, CODEOWNERS
│
├── FILE_MAPPING.md                  # Single source of truth (auto-updated)
├── VERSION                          # v0.1.0
├── CHANGELOG.md                     # Release history
├── CONTRIBUTING.md                  # Branch strategy + PR workflow
└── README.md                        # Repo overview

File Mapping

The FILE_MAPPING.md is the single source of truth for every file in the repo. It auto-updates via GitHub Actions on every merge to main and is used by CI to enforce file placement rules. See the full File Mapping Reference.

docs.title: Built for Kinsta
docs.text:
Every component is designed for WordPress sites hosted on Kinsta:

  • Cache clearing respects Kinsta's infrastructure (no wp kinsta cache purge)
  • SSH-based workflows for remote site management
  • Perfmatters + Kinsta cache coordination
  • Cloudflare CDN-aware performance testing

Ready to get started?

Installation Guide

docs.title: What is the Blaze Dev Kit?
docs.text: The Blaze Dev Kit is a portable .claude/ configuration package that gives Claude Code expert-level WordPress knowledge, automated maintenance workflows, performance optimization tools, and safety guardrails - all pre-configured for Kinsta-hosted sites.

docs.title: Why "Dev Kit" and not just "config" or "setup"?
docs.text: Because it's more than configuration. It includes commands, skills, agents, scripts, and templates - a full toolkit, not just settings.

docs.title: Who maintains the Blaze Dev Kit?
docs.text: The kit is maintained by the Blaze Commerce team. All changes require approval from repository maintainers (Jaru and Jax).

docs.title: Why can't I just clone the repo?
docs.text: The repository contains documentation, QA tests, CI/CD workflows, and maintenance scripts that should NOT be on your WordPress site. The release zip contains ONLY the deployable .claude/ directory - nothing else.

docs.title: What happens if I forget to delete the zip file?
docs.text: A zip file in your public root is a security risk - anyone who knows the URL could download it. Always delete immediately after extraction.

docs.title: Can I install the kit on a non-Kinsta host?
docs.text: The kit is specifically designed for Kinsta. Some components (cache clearing, SSH workflows) are Kinsta-specific. Core skills and commands will work elsewhere, but you'll need to adjust the hosting-specific parts.

docs.title: Does the kit work with any WordPress theme?
docs.text: The kit is optimized for the Blocksy theme (the standard Blaze Commerce stack). Skills and optimization playbooks reference Blocksy-specific patterns. It will work with other themes, but some optimization recommendations may not apply.

docs.title: Why isn't there a CLAUDE.md in the repository?
docs.text: Because every site is different. Instead of a generic CLAUDE.md, the /init command generates a site-specific one by querying your WordPress installation (theme, plugins, WP version, PHP version, etc.). This gives Claude Code accurate context for YOUR site, not a template.

docs.title: Can I customize the generated CLAUDE.md?
docs.text: Yes! After /init generates it, you can add site-specific instructions, client preferences, or custom rules. The updater (update-dev-kit.sh) will never overwrite your CLAUDE.md.

docs.title: What if I need to regenerate CLAUDE.md?
docs.text: Run /init again. It will create a fresh CLAUDE.md based on your current site state. Note: any manual customizations will be replaced. Back up first if needed.

docs.title: How do I know if my site has an old version?
docs.text: Run .claude/scripts/check-version.sh or use the /version-check command inside Claude Code.

docs.title: Will updating delete my files?
docs.text:
No. The update process is non-destructive:

  • Framework files (hooks, skills, commands) are updated
  • Site-specific files (CLAUDE.md, CHANGELOG.md, .env) are preserved
  • A backup is created before changes
  • No files are ever deleted

docs.title: How often are updates released?
docs.text:
There's no fixed schedule. Updates are released when:

  • New skills or commands are added
  • Bug fixes are needed
  • Security improvements are available

Check the CHANGELOG for release history.

docs.title: Can I rollback an update?
docs.text:
Yes. The updater creates a backup at .claude/.backup-v{version}/. To rollback:

rm -rf .claude/
mv .claude/.backup-vX.X.X .claude/

docs.title: What's the difference between commands and skills?
docs.text:
• Commands are user-invoked via slash syntax (/wpm, /pagespeed). They define a specific workflow.
  • Skills are knowledge modules that Claude loads automatically based on the task context. You don't invoke them directly.

docs.title: What's the difference between autonomous and recommend modes?
docs.text:
• Autonomous (/pagespeed): Claude implements fixes automatically in a test-fix-retest loop
  • Recommend (/pagespeed-recommend): Claude analyzes and reports recommendations but makes no changes

docs.title: Can I add custom commands?
docs.text: Yes. Create a .md file in .claude/commands/ following the existing format. Note that custom commands may be overwritten on kit updates unless you're careful with the update process.

docs.title: What are agents and when are they used?
docs.text: Agents are specialist AI subprocesses spawned for specific tasks. Claude Code invokes them automatically when it detects a task matching the agent's expertise. For example, a WooCommerce question might spawn the wp-woo-specialist agent.

docs.title: Why do different agents use different models?
docs.text:
Cost and capability optimization:

  • haiku (cheap, fast): Quick read-only audits
  • sonnet (balanced): Everyday development and optimization
  • opus (powerful, expensive): Complex architecture and migration decisions

docs.title: Do agents inherit safety hooks?
docs.text: Yes. All agents operate within the same safety guardrails (block-dangerous-commands, block-protected-files).

docs.title: What do the safety hooks block?
docs.text:
block-dangerous-commands.sh blocks: rm -rf /, DROP DATABASE, TRUNCATE TABLE, wp db reset, wp plugin delete --all, mkfs., > /dev/sda, wp kinsta cache purge

block-protected-files.sh blocks edits to: wp-includes/, wp-admin/, wp-config.php, parent theme directories, .htaccess, php.ini, .user.ini

docs.title: Can Claude read my .env file?
docs.text: No. The safety hooks prevent Claude from reading .env files and other sensitive credentials.

docs.title: Can I disable the safety hooks?
docs.text: Technically yes (by removing them from settings.json), but strongly discouraged. The hooks exist to prevent accidental damage to production sites.

docs.title: How do I contribute changes to the kit?
docs.text: See the Contributing workflow. In short: create a branch off develop, make changes in the correct directory, create a PR, get approval from a CODEOWNER.

docs.title: Why was my PR rejected by CI?
docs.text:
The verify-pr.yml check enforces file placement rules. Make sure your files are in the correct directory:

  • Kit files → deploy/
  • Documentation → docs/
  • Tests → qa/
  • CI/repo scripts → .github/ or scripts/

docs.title: Can I push directly to main?
docs.text: No. main and develop are protected branches. All changes require a PR with CODEOWNER approval.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to the Blaze Dev Kit. This section walks you through everything you need to deploy the kit to a Kinsta-hosted WordPress site.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Requirements - What you need before starting
  2. Installation - Download, upload, extract, clean up
  3. Initialization - Run /init to configure Claude Code for your site

docs.title: Quick Overview
docs.text:
The Blaze Dev Kit is a .claude/ directory that you deploy to the root of your WordPress site on Kinsta. After extraction, you run /init inside Claude Code, which generates a site-specific CLAUDE.md file with all your site's details, available commands, and safety rules.

From that point on, Claude Code has full knowledge of your site and can perform maintenance, optimization, development, and auditing tasks safely.

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text: After installing the Blaze Dev Kit, run /init to configure Claude Code for your specific site.

docs.title: What /init Does
docs.text:
The /init command performs deterministic site triage and generates a tailored CLAUDE.md file:

  1. Detects your environment via WP-CLI:

    • WordPress version
    • PHP version
    • Active theme and child theme
    • All installed plugins (active and inactive)
    • Site URL and name
    • WooCommerce status (if applicable)
    • Multisite status

  2. Reads service-specific base templates from .claude/templates/:

    • CLAUDE-BASE-WPM.md - Maintenance instructions
    • CLAUDE-BASE-PERFORMANCE.md - Optimization playbooks
    • CLAUDE-BASE-WEBDEV.md - Development conventions
    • CLAUDE-BASE-SEO.md - SEO guidelines

  3. Generates CLAUDE.md in the site root with:

    • Site identity and environment details
    • Available commands, skills, and agents
    • Safety rules and blocked operations
    • Blaze Commerce conventions
    • Link to full documentation
    • Kit version installed

  4. Generates CHANGELOG.md in the site root for tracking all changes made to the site

docs.title: Running /init
docs.text:
# SSH into your Kinsta site, navigate to site root
cd ~/public

# Start Claude Code
claude

# Run initialization
/init

docs.title: Generated Files
docs.text:
After /init, your site root will have:

~/public/
├── CLAUDE.md           # <-- Generated site-specific config
├── CHANGELOG.md        # <-- Generated site change log
├── .claude/            # The Blaze Dev Kit
├── wp-config.php
└── ...

docs.title: Re-running /init
docs.text:
You can safely re-run /init at any time to:

  • Refresh site details after plugin/theme changes
  • Pick up new commands or skills after a kit update
  • Reset CLAUDE.md if it was accidentally modified

CHANGELOG.md is preserved

Re-running /init will regenerate CLAUDE.md but will not overwrite an existing CHANGELOG.md. The changelog is append-only.

docs.title: What Happens Next
docs.text:
After initialization, Claude Code is fully configured. You can:

  • Run /wpm to perform WordPress maintenance updates
  • Run /pagespeed https://your-site.com to optimize performance
  • Run /accessibility https://your-site.com to fix accessibility issues
  • Ask Claude Code any WordPress development question - it has 14 specialist skills loaded

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Step-by-step guide to deploying the Blaze Dev Kit to a Kinsta-hosted WordPress site.

docs.title: Step 1: Download the Latest Release
docs.text:
Go to the Releases page and download the latest blaze-dev-kit-vX.X.X.zip.

Download, don't clone

Do NOT clone the repository. The repo contains documentation, QA tests, and CI/CD files that should not be on your site. Only the release zip contains the deployable kit.

docs.title: Step 2: Upload to Kinsta
docs.text:
Upload the zip file to your site's public root directory (~/public/) using one of these methods:

SFTPSCP (Terminal)SSH + wget
  1. Connect to your Kinsta site via SFTP (credentials in MyKinsta dashboard)
  2. Navigate to the public/ directory
  3. Upload blaze-dev-kit-vX.X.X.zip
scp blaze-dev-kit-vX.X.X.zip user@your-site.kinsta.cloud:~/public/

ssh user@your-site.kinsta.cloud
cd ~/public
wget https://github.com/blaze-commerce/blaze-dev-kit/releases/download/vX.X.X/blaze-dev-kit-vX.X.X.zip

docs.title: Step 3: Extract the Zip
docs.text:
SSH into your Kinsta site and extract:

cd ~/public
unzip blaze-dev-kit-vX.X.X.zip

This creates the .claude/ directory in your site root:

~/public/
├── .claude/           # <-- The Blaze Dev Kit
│   ├── settings.json
│   ├── hooks/
│   ├── commands/
│   ├── skills/
│   ├── agents/
│   ├── scripts/
│   └── templates/
├── wp-config.php
├── wp-content/
└── ...

docs.title: Step 4: Verify Extraction
docs.text:
ls -la .claude/

You should see settings.json, hooks/, commands/, skills/, agents/, scripts/, and templates/.

docs.title: Step 5: Delete the Zip
docs.text:
Do NOT leave the zip file in your public directory.

rm blaze-dev-kit-vX.X.X.zip

Security

A zip file sitting in your public root is a security risk. Always delete it immediately after extraction.

docs.title: Step 6: Initialize
docs.text: See the Initialization guide for the next step.

docs.title: Verification Checklist
docs.text:
• .claude/ directory exists in site root
  • VERSION file inside .claude/ shows correct version
  • Zip file is deleted from public root
  • .claude/scripts/ has executable permissions (chmod +x .claude/scripts/*.sh)

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Before deploying the Blaze Dev Kit, ensure you have the following.

docs.title: Required
docs.text: Requirement Details Claude Code Installed on your local machine (claude.ai/claude-code) Kinsta Hosting Active Kinsta hosting account with SSH access SSH Access SSH credentials for the target site SFTP Client For uploading the zip file (or use scp via terminal) WP-CLI Available on the Kinsta server (pre-installed on all Kinsta sites)

docs.title: Recommended
docs.text: Requirement Details PageSpeed API Key For /pagespeed command - Get one here Browserless Token For validate.sh script - browserless.io Slack Webhook URL For /wpm Slack notifications

docs.title: Standard Site Stack
docs.text:
The kit is optimized for sites running this stack:

  • WordPress 6.4+
  • PHP 8.0+
  • Theme: Blocksy (or Blocksy Pro)
  • Performance: Perfmatters
  • Images: ShortPixel
  • E-commerce: WooCommerce (if applicable)
  • Hosting: Kinsta
  • CDN: Cloudflare (if applicable)

Other stacks

The kit works with other themes and plugins, but the optimization playbooks and automation scripts are specifically tuned for the standard Blaze Commerce stack above.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Some scripts require API keys stored in a .env file at the site root:

# .env (create at site root, add to .gitignore)
PAGESPEED_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
BROWSERLESS_TOKEN=your-token-here
SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL=https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx/yyy/zzz

Security

Never commit .env files. The safety hooks will block Claude from reading .env files directly.

docs.title: Reference
docs.text:
Complete reference for all components in the Blaze Dev Kit.

Section Contents Commands All slash commands and their usage Skills Specialist knowledge modules Agents AI subagents with model routing Scripts Shell tools and automation File Mapping Complete file inventory and deployment categories

docs.title: Agents Reference
docs.text: AI subagents in the Blaze Dev Kit, organized by model tier.

docs.title: What Are Agents?
docs.text:
Agents are specialist AI subprocesses that Claude Code spawns for specific tasks. Each agent has:

  • Restricted tool access (principle of least privilege)
  • Assigned model tier (opus for deep reasoning, sonnet for everyday, haiku for quick tasks)
  • Domain expertise defined in its instruction file

docs.title: Model Routing
docs.text: Model Cost Speed Use Case opus High Slower Architecture, security, complex migrations sonnet Medium Balanced Everyday development, optimization haiku Low Fast Quick audits, lookups, simple analysis

docs.title: Orchestrator
docs.text: Agent Model Tools Purpose wp-orchestrator opus All Meta-agent that routes complex tasks to the right specialist

docs.title: Auditors (Read-Only)
docs.text: Agent Model Tools Purpose wp-theme-auditor haiku Read, Grep, Glob Theme structure analysis, standards compliance wp-plugin-auditor haiku Read, Grep, Glob Plugin code review, security patterns

docs.title: Specialists
docs.text: Agent Model Tools Purpose wp-security-auditor sonnet Read, Grep, Glob, Bash Vulnerability scanning, security hardening wp-performance-optimizer sonnet Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep Performance implementation wp-woo-specialist sonnet Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep WooCommerce development wp-migration-specialist opus All Site migrations, data imports, URL mapping

docs.title: How Agents Are Invoked
docs.text:
Agents are invoked automatically by Claude Code when it determines a task matches the agent's description. You can also reference them explicitly:

"Use the wp-security-auditor agent to scan this plugin for vulnerabilities"

The wp-orchestrator acts as the default router for complex tasks, delegating to the appropriate specialist.

docs.title: Agent File Format
docs.text:
Each agent is a single .md file in .claude/agents/:

---
name: agent-name
description: "When to invoke this agent"
tools: Read, Grep, Glob
model: haiku
---

You are a senior [role] specializing in [domain]...

## Initialization Protocol
## Procedure
## Verification
## Integration with Other Agents

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Auditor agents only have read access - they cannot modify files
  • Specialist agents have write access scoped to their domain
  • All agents inherit the safety hooks (block-dangerous-commands, block-protected-files)

docs.title: Commands Reference
docs.text: All slash commands available in the Blaze Dev Kit.

docs.title: /init
docs.text:
Generate a site-specific CLAUDE.md file.

/init
  • When to use: After fresh installation or after a kit update
  • What it does: Runs WP-CLI triage, generates CLAUDE.md and CHANGELOG.md
  • Safe to re-run: Yes (CHANGELOG.md is preserved)

docs.title: /version-check
docs.text:
Check if your kit is up to date.

/version-check
  • When to use: To check if a newer version is available
  • What it does: Compares local VERSION against latest GitHub Release

docs.title: /wpm
docs.text:
Run the full WordPress maintenance update workflow.

/wpm
  • When to use: Scheduled maintenance windows
  • What it does: Enables maintenance mode, updates core/plugins/themes, disables maintenance mode, notifies Slack
  • Duration: 5-15 minutes depending on update count

docs.title: /pagespeed {url}
docs.text:
Autonomous performance optimization.

/pagespeed https://your-site.com
  • When to use: When PageSpeed score needs improvement
  • What it does: Tests, analyzes, implements fixes, clears cache, re-tests (up to 5 rounds)
  • Target: 90+ mobile PageSpeed score

docs.title: /pagespeed-recommend {url}
docs.text:
Advisory performance audit (no changes made).

/pagespeed-recommend https://your-site.com

docs.title: /accessibility {url}
docs.text:
Autonomous accessibility optimization.

/accessibility https://your-site.com
  • Target: 90+ accessibility score
  • Max rounds: 3

docs.title: /accessibility-recommend {url}
docs.text:
Advisory accessibility audit (no changes made).

/accessibility-recommend https://your-site.com

docs.title: /bestpractices {url}
docs.text:
Autonomous best practices optimization.

/bestpractices https://your-site.com
  • Target: 90+ best practices score
  • Max rounds: 3

docs.title: /bestpractices-recommend {url}
docs.text:
Advisory best practices audit (no changes made).

/bestpractices-recommend https://your-site.com

docs.title: Command Comparison
docs.text: Command Mode Makes Changes? Max Rounds /pagespeed Autonomous Yes 5 /pagespeed-recommend Advisory No 1 /accessibility Autonomous Yes 3 /accessibility-recommend Advisory No 1 /bestpractices Autonomous Yes 3 /bestpractices-recommend Advisory No 1

docs.title: File Mapping
docs.text: The FILE_MAPPING.md in the repository root is the single source of truth for every file in the Blaze Dev Kit.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The file mapping serves three critical functions:

  1. Deploy Verification - verify-deploy-zip.sh validates that the release zip matches the [DEPLOY] files listed in FILE_MAPPING.md
  2. PR Enforcement - verify-pr.yml CI check rejects PRs that place files in the wrong directory
  3. Version QA - When auditing a site installation, compare against FILE_MAPPING.md to detect missing or extra files

docs.title: Categories
docs.text: Label Directory Destination [DEPLOY] deploy/ Kinsta sites (via release zip) [DOCS] docs/ GitHub Pages (MkDocs Material) [QA] qa/ Local Playwright tests (never deployed) [REPO] Root, .github/, scripts/, eval/ Repository only (never deployed)

docs.title: Auto-Update
docs.text: FILE_MAPPING.md is automatically regenerated by the update-mapping.yml GitHub Action on every merge to main. This ensures it never goes stale.

docs.title: As a Developer
docs.text: Before adding a new file, check FILE_MAPPING.md to understand: - Which directory it belongs in - What category it falls under - Whether a similar file already exists

docs.title: As a Maintainer
docs.text: When reviewing PRs, use FILE_MAPPING.md to verify: - New files are in the correct directory - Removed files are reflected in the mapping - No files are in unexpected locations

docs.title: For Site Auditing
docs.text: Compare a site's .claude/ contents against the [DEPLOY] section to find: - Missing files that should be present - Extra files that shouldn't be there - Version mismatches

docs.title: Scripts Reference
docs.text: All shell scripts included in the Blaze Dev Kit.

docs.title: blz-wpm.sh
docs.text:
Main WordPress maintenance automation via SSH.

.claude/scripts/blz-wpm.sh
  • Manages maintenance mode (3-tier priority)
  • Runs core, plugin, and theme updates
  • Sends Slack alerts on failure
  • Excludes premium plugins (handled separately)

docs.title: update-premium-plugins.sh
docs.text:
Premium plugin management from private Git repository.

.claude/scripts/update-premium-plugins.sh [sync|list|update|update-all]
Mode Action sync Sync local premium plugins with repo list List available premium plugins update <name> Update a specific premium plugin update-all Update all premium plugins

docs.title: pagespeed.sh
docs.text:
PageSpeed Insights API testing with multi-run averaging.

.claude/scripts/pagespeed.sh <url> [runs]
  • Default: 5 runs
  • Outputs formatted score tables
  • Saves JSON reports to ./lighthouse-reports/
  • Requires PAGESPEED_API_KEY in .env

docs.title: accessibility.sh
docs.text:
Accessibility audit via PSI API.

.claude/scripts/accessibility.sh <url>
  • Single run (deterministic scores)
  • Groups findings by ARIA, Contrast, Navigation, etc.
  • Saves accessibility-report.json

docs.title: bestpractices.sh
docs.text:
Best practices audit via PSI API.

.claude/scripts/bestpractices.sh <url>
  • Single run
  • Groups by Trust & Safety, UX, Browser Compatibility
  • Saves bestpractices-report.json

docs.title: validate.sh
docs.text:
Live page validation via Browserless.io headless Chrome.

.claude/scripts/validate.sh <url> <mode>
Mode Checks dom Alt text, headings, form labels, ARIA console Console errors, script inventory images Aspect ratios, dimensions, oversized network Mixed content, security headers, third-party screenshot Capture PNG (mobile, desktop, full-page) block Resource blocking experiment all dom + console + images + network

Requires BROWSERLESS_TOKEN in .env.

docs.title: clear-cache.sh
docs.text:
Perfmatters + Kinsta cache clearing and warming.

.claude/scripts/clear-cache.sh
  1. Clears Perfmatters used CSS, local fonts, minified JS/CSS
  2. Clears Kinsta cache
  3. Waits and verifies X-Kinsta-Cache: HIT
  4. Warms Cloudflare cache via PageSpeed test

docs.title: check-version.sh
docs.text:
Compare local version against latest GitHub Release.

.claude/scripts/check-version.sh

docs.title: update-dev-kit.sh
docs.text:
Non-destructive self-updater.

.claude/scripts/update-dev-kit.sh
  • Downloads latest release zip
  • Backs up current .claude/
  • Extracts with rsync (preserves site-specific files)
  • Reports changes

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Scripts that require API keys read from .env at the site root:

Variable Used By PAGESPEED_API_KEY pagespeed.sh, clear-cache.sh BROWSERLESS_TOKEN validate.sh SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL blz-wpm.sh

docs.title: Skills Reference
docs.text: All specialist knowledge modules in the Blaze Dev Kit.

docs.title: Skill Structure
docs.text:
Each skill follows a 3-layer pattern:

skills/<skill-name>/
├── SKILL.md          # Short procedural checklist (loaded first)
├── references/       # Deep-dive documentation (loaded on demand)
└── scripts/          # Deterministic helpers (run, don't reason)

Every SKILL.md includes YAML frontmatter and mandatory sections:

  • When to use - Trigger conditions
  • Inputs required - What's needed before starting
  • Procedure - Step-by-step checklist
  • Verification - How to confirm success
  • Failure modes - Common problems and fixes
  • Escalation - When to ask a human

docs.title: wordpress-router
docs.text:
The entry-point skill that classifies the project and routes to the correct specialist.

  1. Runs deterministic triage (detect-wp-project.sh)
  2. Classifies project type, theme, plugins, PHP version
  3. Routes to the appropriate skill based on task + project type
  4. Intent overrides classification (e.g., REST API question in a plugin project → wp-rest-api)

docs.title: Core Skills
docs.text: Skill Expertise wordpress-master WordPress/WooCommerce architecture, hooks, Blocksy theme, caching php-pro PHP 7.4-8.3+, WordPress coding standards, security patterns security-auditor SQLi, XSS, CSRF, privilege escalation, file permission audits database-administrator MySQL optimization, wp_options, transients, revision management senior-architect Architectural decision framework, code review, trade-off evaluation

docs.title: WordPress-Specific Skills
docs.text: Skill Expertise wp-performance Performance diagnostics, object caching, Core Web Vitals wp-block-development Gutenberg blocks, apiVersion 3, block.json wp-block-themes Block themes, theme.json, templates, style variations wp-plugin-development Plugin architecture, Settings API, data storage wp-rest-api REST endpoints, authentication (cookie/app passwords/OAuth) wp-interactivity-api data-wp-* directives, stores, state synchronization wp-wpcli-ops WP-CLI operations, domain migration, multisite admin wp-playground WordPress Playground for rapid local testing

docs.title: How Skills Load
docs.text:
Skills use progressive disclosure to respect context window limits:

  1. Claude reads SKILL.md (short checklist) first
  2. Deep-dive references/ are only loaded when the task requires them
  3. scripts/ are executed, not reasoned about

This keeps token usage efficient while maintaining access to comprehensive knowledge.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text: The Blaze Dev Kit development roadmap. Items are organized by status and priority.

docs.title: Completed
docs.text:
Released in v0.1.0

  • Unified Repository - Merged claude-wpm and blz-performance into single repo
  • Deploy/Docs Separation - Clear boundary between deployable kit and documentation
  • Safety Hooks - block-dangerous-commands.sh and block-protected-files.sh
  • WPM Command - /wpm for full maintenance workflow
  • Performance Commands - /pagespeed, /accessibility, /bestpractices (autonomous + advisory)
  • 14 Specialist Skills - WordPress, PHP, security, database, architecture, and more
  • 7 AI Agents - Orchestrator, auditors, and specialists with model routing
  • Dynamic /init - Auto-generates site-specific CLAUDE.md via WP-CLI triage
  • Non-Destructive Updates - update-dev-kit.sh with backup and rsync
  • MkDocs Material Docs - Full documentation on GitHub Pages
  • FILE_MAPPING.md - Auto-updated single source of truth
  • Branch Protection - CODEOWNERS, PR requirements, CI enforcement
  • Version Tracking - check-version.sh for site version comparison

docs.title: In Progress
docs.text:
Currently Being Worked On

  • Migrate Existing Sites - Update all current Kinsta sites from old repos to Blaze Dev Kit v0.1.0
  • QA Framework Integration - Port Playwright visual regression tests from claude-wpm
  • Eval Scenarios - JSON test files for validating skill behavior
  • Premium Plugin Registry - Formalize the private premium plugin repo workflow

docs.title: Planned - Next Release (v0.2.0)
docs.text:
Targeting Q2 2026

  • /seo-audit Command - Dedicated technical SEO auditing command
  • wp-seo-specialist Agent - SEO-focused subagent
  • Scaffold Script - scaffold-skill.sh for consistent new skill creation
  • Per-Site Config - .claude/config.local.json for site-specific overrides (gitignored)
  • Slack Integration Enhancement - Rich notifications with before/after metrics
  • Multi-Site Batch Operations - Run commands across multiple Kinsta sites

docs.title: Planned - Future Releases
docs.text:
Backlog

  • /deploy Command - Automated staging-to-production deployment workflow
  • /backup Command - Kinsta backup management via API
  • WooCommerce Audit - Dedicated /woo-audit for store health checks
  • Client Dashboard - Site health overview across all managed sites
  • Automated Scheduling - Cron-based /wpm execution
  • A/B Performance Testing - Compare optimization strategies
  • Custom Skill Marketplace - Team members can contribute and share custom skills
  • Plugin Compatibility Matrix - Track which plugin versions work together
  • Automated Visual Regression - Trigger QA tests after maintenance updates

docs.title: Ideas & Research
docs.text:
Under Consideration

  • Cross-platform packaging (Cursor, Copilot support alongside Claude Code)
  • MCP server integration for real-time WordPress data
  • AI-powered changelog generation from git diffs
  • Kinsta API integration for direct hosting management
  • Client-facing reports (PDF generation from optimization results)

docs.title: How to Suggest Features
docs.text:
1. Create a branch: feature/your-idea
  2. Add details to the appropriate section in this roadmap
  3. Open a PR to develop
  4. Discuss with the team during review

Or raise it directly with @jarutosurano or @jaborjkath.

docs.title: Services
docs.text:
The Blaze Dev Kit supports four core service areas. Each service has dedicated commands, skills, and templates.

Service Commands Primary Use WordPress Maintenance /wpm Core, plugin, and theme updates Site Optimization /pagespeed, /accessibility, /bestpractices Lighthouse score optimization Web Development Skills + Agents Custom WordPress development SEO & Marketing Skills Technical SEO and performance

Each service has a corresponding base template in .claude/templates/ that gets merged into CLAUDE.md during /init.

docs.title: Web Development
docs.text: The Web Development service provides Claude Code with comprehensive WordPress development expertise through specialist skills and agents.

docs.title: Available Skills
docs.text: Skill Expertise wordpress-master WordPress/WooCommerce architecture, hooks, Blocksy theme php-pro Modern PHP 7.4-8.3+, WordPress coding standards, security security-auditor Full security audit: SQLi, XSS, CSRF, privilege escalation database-administrator MySQL optimization, wp_options, transients, revisions senior-architect Architectural decision framework and code review wp-block-development Gutenberg block creation, apiVersion 3 wp-block-themes Block themes, theme.json, templates, style variations wp-plugin-development Plugin architecture, Settings API, data storage wp-rest-api REST endpoints, authentication strategies wp-interactivity-api Client-side directives, stores, hydration wp-wpcli-ops WP-CLI operations, domain migration, multisite wp-playground WordPress Playground for local testing

docs.title: Available Agents
docs.text: Agent Model Specialty wp-orchestrator opus Routes complex tasks to the right specialist wp-theme-auditor haiku Read-only theme analysis (fast, cheap) wp-plugin-auditor haiku Read-only plugin analysis (fast, cheap) wp-security-auditor sonnet Security vulnerability scanning wp-woo-specialist sonnet WooCommerce-specific development wp-migration-specialist opus Complex site migration workflows

docs.title: Router Pattern
docs.text:
The wordpress-router skill acts as the entry point. When Claude encounters a development task:

  1. Triage script runs - deterministically detects project type, theme, plugins, PHP version
  2. Router classifies - maps the task to the correct skill(s)
  3. Specialist executes - the appropriate skill handles the task

This ensures Claude never guesses your stack - it knows it.

docs.title: Safety Guardrails
docs.text:
All development work is protected by:

  • block-dangerous-commands.sh - Prevents rm -rf, DROP DATABASE, etc.
  • block-protected-files.sh - Prevents editing wp-includes/, wp-admin/, wp-config.php, parent themes

docs.title: Coding Conventions
docs.text:
• Function prefix: blaze_ for general, blaze_a11y_ for accessibility, blaze_bp_ for best practices
  • Child theme modifications only (never edit parent theme)
  • Code Snippets Pro for small customizations
  • Full sanitization, escaping, and nonce verification on all custom code

docs.title: File Mapping
docs.text:
.claude/
├── skills/
│   ├── wordpress-router/               # Entry-point router
│   │   ├── SKILL.md                    # Classifies intent, routes to specialist
│   │   └── scripts/
│   │       └── detect-wp-project.sh    # Deterministic project triage
│   ├── wordpress-master/SKILL.md       # WordPress/WooCommerce architecture
│   ├── php-pro/SKILL.md               # Modern PHP 8.0+, coding standards
│   ├── senior-architect/SKILL.md       # Architectural decisions, code review
│   ├── security-auditor/SKILL.md       # Security audit, OWASP patterns
│   ├── wp-block-development/SKILL.md   # Gutenberg block creation
│   ├── wp-block-themes/SKILL.md        # Block themes, theme.json
│   ├── wp-plugin-development/SKILL.md  # Plugin architecture, Settings API
│   ├── wp-rest-api/SKILL.md            # REST endpoints, authentication
│   ├── wp-interactivity-api/SKILL.md   # Interactivity API directives
│   ├── wp-wpcli-ops/SKILL.md           # WP-CLI operations, multisite
│   └── wp-playground/SKILL.md          # WordPress Playground testing
│
├── agents/
│   ├── wp-orchestrator.md              # Routes tasks to specialists (opus)
│   ├── wp-theme-auditor.md             # Read-only theme analysis (haiku)
│   ├── wp-plugin-auditor.md            # Read-only plugin analysis (haiku)
│   ├── wp-security-auditor.md          # Vulnerability scanning (sonnet)
│   ├── wp-woo-specialist.md            # WooCommerce development (sonnet)
│   └── wp-migration-specialist.md      # Site migration (opus)
│
└── templates/
    └── CLAUDE-BASE-WEBDEV.md           # Web dev service base template

docs.title: Site Optimization
docs.text: The Site Optimization service provides autonomous and advisory performance optimization targeting 90+ mobile Lighthouse scores.

docs.title: Autonomous (Auto-Fix)
docs.text: Command Target /pagespeed {url} Performance score optimization /accessibility {url} Accessibility compliance /bestpractices {url} Best practices compliance

docs.title: Advisory (Recommend Only)
docs.text: Command Target /pagespeed-recommend {url} Performance recommendations without implementation /accessibility-recommend {url} Accessibility audit report /bestpractices-recommend {url} Best practices audit report

docs.title: How Autonomous Optimization Works
docs.text:
The autonomous commands run a loop:

graph TD
    A[Test - 5 PSI runs] --> B[Analyze results]
    B --> C{Score >= 90?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Done - Report results]
    C -->|No| E[Implement fixes]
    E --> F[Clear cache + warm]
    F --> G[Wait for CDN propagation]
    G --> A
    B --> H{Max rounds?}
    H -->|Yes| D
  • PageSpeed: Max 5 rounds, exits on 90+ average
  • Accessibility: Max 3 rounds
  • Best Practices: Max 3 rounds

docs.title: Performance Targets
docs.text: Metric Target Description FCP < 1800ms First Contentful Paint LCP < 2500ms Largest Contentful Paint TBT < 200ms Total Blocking Time CLS < 0.1 Cumulative Layout Shift SI < 3400ms Speed Index

docs.title: Common Fix Priority
docs.text:
1. LCP image preloading
  2. DOM size reduction
  3. JavaScript delay/deferral
  4. CSS optimization
  5. Font loading
  6. Image optimization (AVIF, srcset)
  7. Third-party script management

docs.title: Scripts Used
docs.text: Script Purpose pagespeed.sh PSI API multi-run testing accessibility.sh Accessibility audit via PSI bestpractices.sh Best practices audit via PSI validate.sh Live page validation (DOM, console, images, network) clear-cache.sh Perfmatters + Kinsta cache clearing

docs.title: Proven Results
docs.text:
Site Before After Bird Control Australia 29 90+ CannaClear 86 92 Dancewear UK 60 96

Site-specific case studies will be added as sites are migrated to the Blaze Dev Kit.

docs.title: File Mapping
docs.text:
.claude/
├── commands/
│   ├── pagespeed.md                    # Autonomous PageSpeed optimization
│   ├── pagespeed-recommend.md          # Advisory PageSpeed audit
│   ├── accessibility.md               # Autonomous accessibility optimization
│   ├── accessibility-recommend.md      # Advisory accessibility audit
│   ├── bestpractices.md               # Autonomous best practices optimization
│   └── bestpractices-recommend.md      # Advisory best practices audit
│
├── skills/
│   ├── wp-performance/SKILL.md         # Core Web Vitals diagnostics
│   └── database-administrator/SKILL.md # MySQL/query optimization
│
├── agents/
│   └── wp-performance-optimizer.md     # 90+ Lighthouse implementation
│
├── scripts/
│   ├── pagespeed.sh                    # PSI API multi-run testing
│   ├── accessibility.sh               # Accessibility audit via PSI
│   ├── bestpractices.sh               # Best practices audit via PSI
│   ├── validate.sh                    # Live page validation (DOM, console, images)
│   └── clear-cache.sh                 # Perfmatters + Kinsta cache clearing
│
└── templates/
    └── CLAUDE-BASE-PERFORMANCE.md      # Performance service base template

docs.title: SEO & Marketing
docs.text: The SEO & Marketing service provides technical SEO expertise and optimization guidance for WordPress sites.

docs.title: Current Capabilities
docs.text:
The kit includes SEO knowledge as part of the broader skill set:

  • Technical SEO auditing via the wordpress-master and wp-performance skills
  • Schema markup guidance for WooCommerce products
  • Core Web Vitals optimization (directly tied to PageSpeed optimization)
  • Structured data validation
  • Rank Math plugin configuration (part of the standard Blaze Commerce stack)

docs.title: Integration with Other Services
docs.text:
SEO is deeply connected to other services:

Service SEO Impact Site Optimization Core Web Vitals directly affect search rankings WPM Keeping plugins updated prevents SEO-impacting bugs Web Development Proper heading hierarchy, semantic HTML, alt text

docs.title: CLAUDE-BASE-SEO.md Template
docs.text:
The SEO base template includes guidelines for:

  • Rank Math configuration best practices
  • Schema markup patterns for products, articles, local business
  • XML sitemap optimization
  • Robots.txt configuration
  • Canonical URL management
  • Open Graph and Twitter Card meta
  • Internal linking structure
  • Image SEO (alt text, file naming, compression)

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
Planned Enhancements

Dedicated /seo-audit command and wp-seo-specialist agent are planned for future releases. See the Roadmap for details.

docs.title: File Mapping
docs.text:
.claude/
├── skills/
│   ├── wp-performance/SKILL.md         # Core Web Vitals (shared with Optimization)
│   └── wordpress-master/SKILL.md       # Technical SEO via WP architecture (shared)
│
└── templates/
    └── CLAUDE-BASE-SEO.md              # SEO service base template (Rank Math, schema, meta)

docs.title: WordPress Maintenance (WPM)
docs.text: The WordPress Maintenance service automates the full update workflow for Kinsta-hosted WordPress sites.

docs.title: Command: /wpm
docs.text:
Runs the complete maintenance update workflow:

  1. Enable maintenance mode (3-tier priority):
    • WooCommerce Coming Soon page (preferred for WooCommerce sites)
    • ASE Pro maintenance mode
    • Custom .maintenance file (fallback)
  2. Update WordPress core
  3. Run database updates (wp core update-db)
  4. Update free plugins (excluding premium)
  5. Update premium plugins (from private repository)
  6. Update themes
  7. Disable maintenance mode
  8. Update plugin inventory in CLAUDE.md
  9. Notify Slack (optional)

docs.title: Why Maintenance Mode?
docs.text:
Critical for WooCommerce

WooCommerce sites must be in maintenance mode during updates. Without it, customers may experience failed orders, broken checkout, or payment processing errors during the update window.

docs.title: Premium Plugin Management
docs.text:
Premium plugins are managed separately from a private Git repository. The /wpm command handles:

  • Elementor Pro
  • GP Premium
  • Perfmatters
  • WooCommerce Checkout Field Editor Pro
  • Admin & Site Enhancements Pro
  • WP Mail SMTP Pro
  • SureRank Pro

docs.title: Scripts Used
docs.text: Script Purpose blz-wpm.sh Main maintenance automation update-premium-plugins.sh Premium plugin sync from private repo notify-slack.sh Slack notifications

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
The WPM base template (CLAUDE-BASE-WPM.md) includes:

  • Maintenance mode priority rules
  • Plugin update exclusion lists
  • Slack webhook configuration
  • Post-update verification steps
  • Kinsta cache clearing procedures

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Always run /wpm during low-traffic hours
  2. Check the site frontend after updates complete
  3. Review the CHANGELOG.md for what was updated
  4. If issues arise, the maintenance mode can be manually disabled via WP-CLI

docs.title: File Mapping
docs.text:
.claude/
├── commands/
│   ├── wpm.md                          # Full maintenance workflow
│   ├── init.md                         # Initialize site-specific config
│   └── version-check.md               # Check kit version
│
├── skills/
│   ├── wordpress-master/SKILL.md       # WordPress/WooCommerce architecture
│   └── wp-wpcli-ops/SKILL.md          # WP-CLI operations, domain migration
│
├── agents/
│   ├── wp-orchestrator.md              # Routes complex tasks to specialists
│   └── wp-migration-specialist.md      # Site migration workflows
│
├── scripts/
│   ├── blz-wpm.sh                      # Main maintenance automation
│   ├── update-premium-plugins.sh       # Premium plugin sync from private repo
│   ├── check-version.sh               # Version comparison against GitHub
│   └── update-dev-kit.sh              # Non-destructive self-updater
│
├── templates/
│   ├── CLAUDE.md.template              # Site-specific CLAUDE.md generator
│   ├── CLAUDE-BASE-WPM.md             # WPM service base template
│   └── CHANGELOG.md.template          # Changelog template
│
└── hooks/PreToolUse/
    ├── block-dangerous-commands.sh     # Blocks destructive commands
    └── block-protected-files.sh        # Prevents WP core edits

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text:
Documented procedures for every aspect of the Blaze Dev Kit lifecycle.

Workflow Audience Purpose Developer Setup All developers Deploy the kit to a new site Updating Sites All developers Update an existing installation Contributing Kit contributors How to make changes to the kit Releasing Maintainers only How to cut a new version GitHub Pages Maintainers only How documentation deployment works Migration Guide Existing users Migrate from claude-wpm or blz-performance

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: How to contribute changes to the Blaze Dev Kit repository.

docs.title: Branch Strategy
docs.text:
graph LR
    F[feature/branch] -->|PR| D[develop]
    D -->|PR when stable| M[main]
    M -->|tag v2.1.0| R[Release]
Branch Purpose Protection main Production-ready releases PR + CODEOWNER approval required develop Integration and testing PR + 1 approval required feature/* Individual work No protection fix/* Bug fixes No protection docs/* Documentation changes No protection chore/* Maintenance tasks No protection

docs.title: 1. Create Your Branch
docs.text:
git checkout develop
git pull origin develop
git checkout -b feature/your-description

docs.title: 2. Make Changes in the Correct Directory
docs.text:
Change Type Directory Kit files (hooks, skills, commands, etc.) deploy/ Documentation docs/ Tests qa/ CI/CD, repo scripts .github/, scripts/

File Placement is Enforced

The verify-pr.yml GitHub Action will reject your PR if files are in the wrong directory. Check FILE_MAPPING.md if unsure.

docs.title: 3. Commit with Conventional Commits
docs.text:
git commit -m "feat(wpm): add Slack notification for failed updates"
git commit -m "fix(hooks): correct regex in block-protected-files"
git commit -m "docs(installation): add Kinsta SSH troubleshooting"

docs.title: 4. Push and Create PR
docs.text:
git push -u origin feature/your-description

Then create a PR to develop using the PR template.

docs.title: 5. Get Approval
docs.text:
All PRs require approval from a CODEOWNER:

  • @jarutosurano (Jaru)
  • @jaborjkath (Jax)

docs.title: 6. Merge
docs.text: After approval + CI checks pass, the PR can be merged. Your branch auto-deletes after merge.

docs.title: Adding a New Skill
docs.text:
Use the scaffold script:

./scripts/scaffold-skill.sh wp-new-skill

This generates the proper structure:

deploy/.claude/skills/wp-new-skill/
├── SKILL.md          # With YAML frontmatter and required sections
└── references/       # For deep-dive docs (optional)

docs.title: Code Standards
docs.text:
• Shell scripts: shellcheck compliant, set -euo pipefail
  • Skills: YAML frontmatter + mandatory sections (When to use, Procedure, Verification, Failure modes, Escalation)
  • Agents: YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model) + structured body
  • Docs: MkDocs Material markdown with proper frontmatter

docs.title: Developer Setup Workflow
docs.text: Complete workflow for deploying the Blaze Dev Kit to a new Kinsta-hosted WordPress site.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Claude Code installed on your machine
  • SSH access to the Kinsta site
  • SFTP client or terminal access
  • PageSpeed API key (for optimization commands)

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[1. Download release zip] --> B[2. Upload to Kinsta]
    B --> C[3. SSH into site]
    C --> D[4. Extract zip]
    D --> E[5. Verify .claude/ exists]
    E --> F[6. Delete zip file]
    F --> G[7. Set script permissions]
    G --> H[8. Start Claude Code]
    H --> I[9. Run /init]
    I --> J[10. Verify CLAUDE.md generated]
    J --> K[Ready to work!]

docs.title: 1. Download the Release
docs.text:
Go to github.com/blaze-commerce/blaze-dev-kit/releases and download the latest .zip file.

Do NOT clone the repo

The repository contains docs, QA tests, and CI files that should NOT be on your site. Always use the release zip.

docs.title: 2. Upload to Kinsta
docs.text: scp blaze-dev-kit-v0.1.0.zip user@site.kinsta.cloud:~/public/

docs.title: 3. SSH and Extract
docs.text:
ssh user@site.kinsta.cloud
cd ~/public
unzip blaze-dev-kit-v0.1.0.zip

docs.title: 4. Verify and Clean Up
docs.text:
# Verify extraction
ls -la .claude/
cat .claude/../VERSION 2>/dev/null || cat .claude/settings.json | head -1

# Delete the zip (MANDATORY)
rm blaze-dev-kit-v0.1.0.zip

# Set executable permissions on scripts
chmod +x .claude/scripts/*.sh

docs.title: 5. Initialize Claude Code
docs.text:
# Start Claude Code in the site directory
claude

# Run initialization
/init

docs.title: 6. Verify Initialization
docs.text:
After /init completes, verify:

  • CLAUDE.md exists in site root
  • CHANGELOG.md exists in site root
  • CLAUDE.md contains your site name and URL
  • CLAUDE.md lists your active theme and plugins

docs.title: 7. Test a Command
docs.text:
# Quick test - check version
/version-check

# Or run a PageSpeed test
/pagespeed https://your-site.com

docs.title: "Command not found" when running /init
docs.text: Ensure you're running Claude Code from the site root directory where .claude/ is located.

docs.title: Scripts not executable
docs.text: chmod +x .claude/scripts/*.sh

docs.title: WP-CLI not available
docs.text: WP-CLI is pre-installed on all Kinsta sites. If it's not working, check your SSH connection is to the correct environment (staging vs production).

docs.title: GitHub Pages Workflow
docs.text: How the documentation site is built and deployed.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Blaze Dev Kit documentation is built with MkDocs Material and deployed to GitHub Pages automatically on every merge to main.

Live site: blaze-commerce.github.io/blaze-dev-kit

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Merge to main] --> B[deploy-docs.yml triggers]
    B --> C[Install Python + MkDocs]
    C --> D[Build static site]
    D --> E[Deploy to gh-pages branch]
    E --> F[Site live on GitHub Pages]

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
cd docs/
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Preview
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

This starts a local server at http://127.0.0.1:8000/ with live reload.

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
mkdocs build

Output goes to docs/site/ (gitignored).

docs.title: Making Documentation Changes
docs.text:
1. Create a docs/ branch: git checkout -b docs/update-installation
  2. Edit files in docs/docs/
  3. Preview locally with mkdocs serve
  4. Commit and create PR to develop
  5. After merge to develop → main, the site auto-deploys

docs.title: Adding New Pages
docs.text:
1. Create the .md file in the appropriate directory under docs/docs/
  2. Add the page to the nav section in docs/mkdocs.yml
  3. Update FILE_MAPPING.md if needed

docs.title: Private Repo + Public Docs
docs.text:
The repository is private but GitHub Pages is public. This means:

  • Source code, skills, hooks, scripts = private (repo access only)
  • Documentation website = publicly accessible

This is the expected behavior on GitHub Pro. If you need the docs site itself to be private (restricted to team members only), that requires GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

What NOT to put in docs

Never include API keys, credentials, internal URLs, or sensitive client information in the documentation. The docs site is publicly accessible.

docs.title: Migration Guide
docs.text: How to migrate from claude-wpm or blz-performance to the unified Blaze Dev Kit.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Blaze Dev Kit (v0.1.0) is the unified successor to two separate repositories:

Old Repository Service Status blaze-commerce/claude-wpm WordPress Maintenance Merged into Blaze Dev Kit blaze-commerce/blz-performance Site Optimization Merged into Blaze Dev Kit

docs.title: Merged Components
docs.text: Component claude-wpm blz-performance Blaze Dev Kit Safety hooks 2 hooks 2 hooks (duplicated) 2 hooks (deduplicated) Settings Narrow permissions Broad permissions Unified (merged) Skills 17 files 6 files All combined (deduplicated) Scripts 8 scripts 5 scripts All combined Commands /wpm only None (skills only) All commands unified Agents None None NEW - 7 specialist agents Templates CLAUDE-BASE.md CLAUDE.md Service-specific bases

docs.title: New in v0.1.0
docs.text:
• Subagent architecture with model routing
  • Router pattern for deterministic project classification
  • 3-layer skill documentation structure
  • Dynamic CLAUDE.md generation via /init
  • Per-site CHANGELOG.md generation
  • Non-destructive version update system
  • MkDocs Material documentation site

docs.title: From claude-wpm
docs.text:
1. Backup your current .claude/ directory: 

    cp -r .claude/ .claude-backup-wpm/

  2. Backup your current CLAUDE.md (if customized): 

    cp CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md.backup

  3. Remove the old .claude/ directory: 

    rm -rf .claude/

  4. Install Blaze Dev Kit following the Installation guide

  5. Run /init to generate new CLAUDE.md

  6. Review the generated CLAUDE.md and merge any custom instructions from your backup

  7. Clean up backups once satisfied: 

    rm -rf .claude-backup-wpm/
rm CLAUDE.md.backup

docs.title: From blz-performance
docs.text: Same steps as above. Your .env file (API keys) is preserved since it's at the site root, not inside .claude/.

docs.title: From Both (Site Had Both Installed)
docs.text: If a site had files from both repos, follow the same steps. The Blaze Dev Kit replaces both completely.

docs.title: Verifying Migration
docs.text:
After migration, verify:

  • /wpm command works
  • /pagespeed https://your-site.com works
  • /version-check shows v0.1.0
  • CLAUDE.md contains site details
  • Safety hooks block rm -rf / (test safely)

docs.title: FAQ
docs.text:
Q: Will I lose my site-specific CLAUDE.md customizations? A: /init generates a fresh CLAUDE.md. If you had custom instructions, back them up first and merge them after.

Q: Are my .env API keys affected? A: No. The .env file is at the site root, outside .claude/. It's never touched.

Q: Can I run the old and new versions side by side? A: No. There should only be one .claude/ directory per site.

docs.title: Releasing
docs.text:
How maintainers cut a new version of the Blaze Dev Kit.

Maintainers Only

Only @jarutosurano and @jaborjkath can create releases.

docs.title: 1. Ensure develop is Stable
docs.text: All features and fixes for this release should be merged to develop and tested.

docs.title: 2. Create Release PR
docs.text:
git checkout develop
git pull origin develop
git checkout -b release/v0.2.0

Update version files: 

echo "v0.2.0" > VERSION
echo "v0.2.0" > deploy/VERSION

Update CHANGELOG.md with the new version section.

git add VERSION deploy/VERSION CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "chore(release): prepare v0.2.0"
git push -u origin release/v0.2.0

Create a PR from release/v0.2.0 → main.

docs.title: 3. Merge to Main
docs.text: After approval and CI checks pass, merge the PR.

docs.title: 4. Tag the Release
docs.text:
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git tag v0.2.0
git push origin v0.2.0

docs.title: 5. Automated Release
docs.text:
Pushing the tag triggers release.yml which:

  1. Runs security checks
  2. Builds the deploy zip from deploy/ directory
  3. Verifies the zip against FILE_MAPPING.md
  4. Creates a GitHub Release with:
    • blaze-dev-kit-v0.2.0.zip attached
    • Changelog excerpt as release notes

docs.title: 6. Merge Back to Develop
docs.text:
git checkout develop
git merge main
git push origin develop

docs.title: Versioning Rules
docs.text: Change Version Bump Example Breaking changes to deploy structure MAJOR v0.1.0 → v1.0.0 New skills, commands, agents, scripts MINOR v0.1.0 → v0.2.0 Bug fixes, doc updates, minor tweaks PATCH v0.1.0 → v0.1.1

docs.title: Deploy Zip Contents
docs.text:
The zip is built from the deploy/ directory only:

# What the release workflow runs:
cd deploy && zip -r ../blaze-dev-kit-v0.2.0.zip .claude/ VERSION

The zip extracts to create .claude/ and VERSION in the target directory.

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
After release:

  1. Download the zip from the release page
  2. Extract and verify structure matches FILE_MAPPING.md
  3. Test on a staging Kinsta site
  4. Run /init and verify CLAUDE.md generation

docs.title: Updating Sites
docs.text: How to update the Blaze Dev Kit on an existing Kinsta site installation.

docs.title: Quick Check
docs.text:
.claude/scripts/check-version.sh

Output: 

Blaze Dev Kit Version Check
Local:  v0.1.0
Latest: v2.1.0
Status: UPDATE AVAILABLE

Run .claude/scripts/update-dev-kit.sh to update.

docs.title: Via Claude Code
docs.text:
claude
/version-check

docs.title: Automatic (Recommended)
docs.text:
.claude/scripts/update-dev-kit.sh

This script performs a non-destructive update:

  1. Downloads the latest release zip from GitHub
  2. Backs up current .claude/ to .claude/.backup-v{current}/
  3. Extracts new files over existing installation
  4. Uses rsync to:
    • Update framework files (hooks, commands, skills, agents, scripts)
    • Preserve site-specific files (CLAUDE.md, local configs)
    • Add new files that didn't exist before
    • Never delete existing files
  5. Updates VERSION to the new version
  6. Reports what changed

docs.title: Manual Update
docs.text:
If the automatic updater isn't working:

  1. Download the latest release zip
  2. Upload to Kinsta site root
  3. Back up your current .claude/ directory
  4. Extract the zip (overwrites .claude/)
  5. Delete the zip
  6. Re-run /init to refresh CLAUDE.md

docs.title: What Gets Preserved
docs.text: File Updated? Notes .claude/settings.json Yes Framework defaults restored .claude/hooks/* Yes Latest safety rules applied .claude/commands/* Yes New/updated commands .claude/skills/* Yes New/updated knowledge .claude/agents/* Yes New/updated agents .claude/scripts/* Yes New/updated tools .claude/templates/* Yes New/updated templates CLAUDE.md (site root) No Site-specific, never touched CHANGELOG.md (site root) No Site history, never touched .env No Credentials, never touched

docs.title: After Updating
docs.text:
After an update, it's recommended to:

  1. Re-run /init to pick up any new commands or skills in your CLAUDE.md
  2. Check the CHANGELOG for what changed
  3. Test a quick command to verify everything works

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text:
If an update causes issues:

# Restore from backup
rm -rf .claude/
mv .claude/.backup-v0.1.0 .claude/

Backups

The updater keeps one backup per version. Updating from v0.1.0 to v0.2.0 creates .claude/.backup-v0.1.0/. Updating again to v0.3.0 creates .claude/.backup-v0.2.0/ (previous backup is removed to save space).
